title: Declarative vs. Imperative Paradigms
description: Declarative programming describes *what* the end state should be without defining the step-by-step execution. Imperative programming describes *how* to achieve a state by defining the explicit control flow and memory mutations. HTML, Markdown, and CSS are strictly declarative languages. They describe structural, semantic, and stylistic intent (e.g., 'Make this a heading', 'Paint this blue'). They do not dictate the algorithmic steps the rendering engine or parser must take to allocate memory, calculate layout geometries, or paint pixels to a screen.
